entities.name: ASCOCHYTA RABIEI
entities.description: A fungal pathogen that causes blight in chickpeas and is the subject of multiplication tests.

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop whose seeds are used as a growth medium for fungal multiplication.

entities.name: SPORE PRODUCTION
entities.description: The capacity of the fungus to produce spores, used as a measure of growth efficiency.

entities.name: LIQUID MEDIA
entities.description: A type of growth medium used as a comparative baseline for spore production.

entities.name: DELHI ISOLATE
entities.description: A specific strain of Ascochyta rabiei originating from Delhi.

entities.name: HISAR ISOLATE
entities.description: A specific strain of Ascochyta rabiei originating from Hisar.

entities.name: KABULI CHICKPEA
entities.description: A specific variety of chickpea used in the most effective growth medium.

entities.name: SUCROSE
entities.description: A sugar added to the growth medium to optimize spore production.

entities.name: DELHI
entities.description: The geographical location from which one isolate of A. rabiei was obtained.

entities.name: HISAR
entities.description: The geographical location from which one isolate of A. rabiei was obtained.

entities.name: GROWTH MEDIUM
entities.description: The substrate used for the laboratory multiplication of Ascochyta rabiei.
